sensitiveSecurityParameters.name: RAM
sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: The module stores ephemeral keys/SSPs in RAM provided by the operational environment. They are received for use or generated by the module only at the command of the calling application. The operating system protects all keys/SSPs through the memory separation and protection mechanisms. No process other than the module itself can access the keys/SSPs in its process’ memory.

services.name: Non- Approved mode
services.description: Non-Approved mode of operation is entered when the module utilizes non- approved security functions in Table 8.
services.indicator: return a '0' from fips_allowed_mode() for block cipher functions and fips_allowed() for all other services to indicate the executed cryptographic algorithm was non-approved

Self-test
services.description: Execute the CASTs

services.name: Show Status
services.description: Return the module status

services.name: Show Module Info
services.description: Return Module Base Name and Module Version Number

services.name: Error State
services.description: The HMAC-SHA-256 value computed over the module did not match the pre-computed value
services.indicator: Error message “FAILED: fipspost_post_integrity” is sent to the caller

services.name: Error State
services.description: The computed value in the invoked Conditional CAST did not match the known value
services.indicator: Error message “FAILED:<event>” sent to the caller (<event> refers to any of the cryptographic functions listed in Table 19.)

services.name: Error State
services.description: The signature failed to verify successfully in the Conditional PCT.
services.indicator: Error message “CCEC_GENERATE_KEY_CONSISTENCY” returned for EC Error message “CCRSA_GENERATE_KEY_CONSISTENCY” returned for RSA Error message “CCDH_GENERATE_KEY_CONSISTENCY” returned for DH

portsAndInterfaces.physicalPort: As a software-only module, the module does not have physical ports. Physical Ports are interpreted to be the physical ports of the hardware platform on which it runs
portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Data inputs are provided in the variables passed in the API and callable service invocations, generally through caller-supplied buffers
portsAndInterfaces.name: As a software-only module, the module does not have physical ports. Physical Ports are interpreted to be the physical ports of the hardware platform on which it runs

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Data outputs are provided in the variables passed in the API and callable service invocations, generally through caller-supplied buffers
portsAndInterfaces.name: Data Output

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Control inputs which control the mode of the module are provided through dedicated parameters.
portsAndInterfaces.name: Control Input

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Status output is provided in return codes and through messages. Documentation for each API lists possible return codes. A complete list of all return codes returned by the C language APIs within the module is provided in the header files and the API documentation. Messages are also documented in the API documentation.
portsAndInterfaces.name: Status Output

The entropy source consists of twenty-four Free Ring Oscillator (FROs) with a vetted conditioning function SHA-256 (ACVP cert. # C1223)
The non-physical entropy source is based upon interrupt timings with a vetted conditioning function SHA-256 (ACVP certs. # A2797, A2869)

The module stores ephemeral keys/SSPs in RAM provided by the operational environment. They are received for use or generated by the module only at the command of the calling application. The operating system protects all keys/SSPs through the memory separation and protection mechanisms. No process other than the module itself can access the keys/SSPs in its process’ memory.
